2026-03-26

# ============================================================================
# ICRG: Healthy Control vs. Recreational Gamblers — Paper Analysis
# Author: Cooper Joseph Sharp
# Date:   2026-03-26
# ============================================================================

library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.3.3
library(car)
## Warning: package 'car' was built under R version 4.3.3
## Loading required package: carData
## 
## Attaching package: 'car'
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     recode
library(broom)
## Warning: package 'broom' was built under R version 4.3.3
library(pscl)
## Classes and Methods for R developed in the
## Political Science Computational Laboratory
## Department of Political Science
## Stanford University
## Simon Jackman
## hurdle and zeroinfl functions by Achim Zeileis
library(lmtest)
## Loading required package: zoo
## 
## Attaching package: 'zoo'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     as.Date, as.Date.numeric
library(sandwich)
library(tidycensus)
## Warning: package 'tidycensus' was built under R version 4.3.3
library(pROC)
## Warning: package 'pROC' was built under R version 4.3.3
## Type 'citation("pROC")' for a citation.
## 
## Attaching package: 'pROC'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     cov, smooth, var
library(knitr)

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 1. DATA LOADING & PREPARATION
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

setwd("~/Documents/GitHub/2025-ICRG")
df     <- read.csv("derivatives/scoring/analytic-sample.csv")

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 2. ANALYTIC SAMPLE — HC (0) vs. Recreational (1)
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

dat <- df %>%
  filter(pg != 2) %>%
  drop_na(
    pg, demo_gender, income_median_split,
    TotalCrime, unemployed, gini, demo_yrs,
    rew_composite,
    dospert_gam, dospert_soc, dospert_inv,
    dospert_hs, dospert_ethic, dospert_rec,
    susd_depression, susd_mania, scared_total,
    uclal_total, bisbas_bas, bisbas_bis, sas_total, planfulness_mean
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    PG     = ifelse(pg == 1, 1L, 0L),      # 0 = HC, 1 = recreational
    gender = factor(demo_gender),
    income = factor(income_median_split),

    z_age           = as.numeric(scale(demo_yrs)),
    z_yoe           = as.numeric(scale(ses_yoe)),
    z_fevs          = as.numeric(scale(fevs_total)),
    z_usidep        = as.numeric(scale(usidep_total)),

    z_reward        = as.numeric(scale(rew_composite)),
    z_dospert_inv   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_inv)),
    z_dospert_ethic = as.numeric(scale(dospert_ethic)),
    z_dospert_soc   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_soc)),
    z_dospert_hs    = as.numeric(scale(dospert_hs)),
    z_dospert_rec   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_rec)),

    z_depression    = as.numeric(scale(susd_depression)),
    z_mania         = as.numeric(scale(susd_mania)),
    z_anxiety       = as.numeric(scale(scared_total)),
    z_loneliness    = as.numeric(scale(uclal_total)),
    z_planfulness   = as.numeric(scale(planfulness_mean)),

    z_neigh_disadv = rowMeans(cbind(
      as.numeric(scale(TotalCrime)),
      as.numeric(scale(unemployed)),
      as.numeric(scale(gini))
    ), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  filter(gender %in% c(1, 2))

cat("Analytic N =", nrow(dat), "\n")
## Analytic N = 621
cat("HC (PG=0):",          sum(dat$PG == 0), "\n")
## HC (PG=0): 378
cat("Recreational (PG=1):", sum(dat$PG == 1), "\n")
## Recreational (PG=1): 243
table(dat$race, dat$PG)
##        
##           0   1
##   Asian  18   7
##   Black  28  48
##   Other  21  13
##   White 311 175
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 3. HIERARCHICAL LOGISTIC REGRESSION
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Block 1: Demographics + neighbourhood context
m1 <- glm(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe,
  data = dat, family = binomial
)

# Block 2: + Reward sensitivity & risk-taking
m2 <- glm(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic +
    z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec,
  data = dat, family = binomial
)

# Block 3: + Clinical/social symptoms
m3 <- glm(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic +
    z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec +
    z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness +
    z_planfulness,
  data = dat, family = binomial
)

# Block 4: + Financial vulnerability
m4 <- glm(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + income + race + z_yoe +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic +
    z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec +
    z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness +
    z_planfulness +
    z_fevs + z_usidep,
  data = dat, family = binomial
)


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 4. MODEL COMPARISONS
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat("\n── Likelihood-ratio tests ──\n")
## 
## ── Likelihood-ratio tests ──
anova(m1, m2, test = "Chisq")
## Analysis of Deviance Table
## 
## Model 1: PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe
## Model 2: PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe + z_reward + 
##     z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic + z_dospert_inv + 
##     z_dospert_rec
##   Resid. Df Resid. Dev Df Deviance  Pr(>Chi)    
## 1       613     758.08                          
## 2       607     720.81  6   37.276 1.556e-06 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(m2, m3, test = "Chisq")
## Analysis of Deviance Table
## 
## Model 1: PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe + z_reward + 
##     z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic + z_dospert_inv + 
##     z_dospert_rec
## Model 2: PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe + z_reward + 
##     z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic + z_dospert_inv + 
##     z_dospert_rec + z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness + 
##     z_planfulness
##   Resid. Df Resid. Dev Df Deviance Pr(>Chi)
## 1       607     720.81                     
## 2       602     713.55  5   7.2617   0.2019
anova(m3, m4, test = "Chisq")
## Analysis of Deviance Table
## 
## Model 1: PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe + z_reward + 
##     z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic + z_dospert_inv + 
##     z_dospert_rec + z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness + 
##     z_planfulness
## Model 2: PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + income + race + z_yoe + 
##     z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic + 
##     z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec + z_depression + z_mania + 
##     z_anxiety + z_loneliness + z_planfulness + z_fevs + z_usidep
##   Resid. Df Resid. Dev Df Deviance Pr(>Chi)
## 1       602     713.55                     
## 2       599     711.61  3   1.9333   0.5864
data.frame(
  Model       = c("M1: Context", "M2: + Reward/Risk",
                   "M3: + Clinical", "M4: + Financial"),
  AIC         = round(AIC(m1, m2, m3, m4)$AIC, 1),
  BIC         = round(BIC(m1, m2, m3, m4)$BIC, 1),
  McFadden_R2 = round(c(pR2(m1)["McFadden"], pR2(m2)["McFadden"],
                         pR2(m3)["McFadden"], pR2(m4)["McFadden"]), 3)
) %>% kable()
## fitting null model for pseudo-r2
## fitting null model for pseudo-r2
## fitting null model for pseudo-r2
## fitting null model for pseudo-r2
Model AIC BIC McFadden_R2
M1: Context 774.1 809.5 0.088
M2: + Reward/Risk 748.8 810.8 0.133
M3: + Clinical 751.5 835.7 0.142
M4: + Financial 755.6 853.1 0.144 
cat("\n── VIF (M4) ──\n")
## 
## ── VIF (M4) ──
vif(m4)
##                     GVIF Df GVIF^(1/(2*Df))
## z_neigh_disadv  1.079590  1        1.039033
## gender          1.144210  1        1.069677
## z_age           2.069868  1        1.438703
## income          1.188356  1        1.090117
## race            1.275836  3        1.041436
## z_yoe           1.276589  1        1.129862
## z_reward        1.494977  1        1.222692
## z_dospert_hs    3.727067  1        1.930561
## z_dospert_soc   3.565865  1        1.888350
## z_dospert_ethic 5.115392  1        2.261723
## z_dospert_inv   3.462293  1        1.860724
## z_dospert_rec   3.817739  1        1.953904
## z_depression    3.186739  1        1.785144
## z_mania         2.092470  1        1.446537
## z_anxiety       2.849073  1        1.687920
## z_loneliness    1.814648  1        1.347089
## z_planfulness   1.594139  1        1.262592
## z_fevs          1.841489  1        1.357015
## z_usidep        1.479002  1        1.216142
cat("\n── HC3 Robust SEs (M4) ──\n")
## 
## ── HC3 Robust SEs (M4) ──
coeftest(m4, vcov = vcovHC(m4, type = "HC3"))
## 
## z test of coefficients:
## 
##                              Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)   
## (Intercept)                -1.1338434  0.5343333 -2.1220  0.03384 * 
## z_neigh_disadv              0.3103408  0.1377114  2.2536  0.02422 * 
## gender2                    -0.2871234  0.2033877 -1.4117  0.15804   
## z_age                      -0.1648399  0.1422509 -1.1588  0.24654   
## incomeBelow Median (<$40k) -0.2275657  0.2134804 -1.0660  0.28643   
## raceBlack                   1.5761216  0.5819303  2.7084  0.00676 **
## raceOther                   0.5337471  0.6563860  0.8132  0.41613   
## raceWhite                   0.9288734  0.5334523  1.7412  0.08164 . 
## z_yoe                       0.0018178  0.1087723  0.0167  0.98667   
## z_reward                    0.3135377  0.1270744  2.4674  0.01361 * 
## z_dospert_hs               -0.1230897  0.1941685 -0.6339  0.52613   
## z_dospert_soc              -0.0332841  0.2038367 -0.1633  0.87029   
## z_dospert_ethic             0.0313267  0.2404242  0.1303  0.89633   
## z_dospert_inv               0.2280222  0.1906154  1.1962  0.23160   
## z_dospert_rec               0.3422955  0.2034897  1.6821  0.09254 . 
## z_depression                0.0502177  0.1791766  0.2803  0.77927   
## z_mania                     0.1173337  0.1416076  0.8286  0.40734   
## z_anxiety                  -0.1851214  0.1654104 -1.1192  0.26307   
## z_loneliness               -0.1376283  0.1272549 -1.0815  0.27947   
## z_planfulness              -0.1837613  0.1246020 -1.4748  0.14027   
## z_fevs                     -0.0057762  0.1351240 -0.0427  0.96590   
## z_usidep                   -0.0659163  0.1274552 -0.5172  0.60504   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 5. ODDS RATIOS
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

col_pg <- "purple4"

get_or_table <- function(model) {
  est <- coef(model)
  ci  <- suppressMessages(confint(model))
  data.frame(
    term    = names(est),
    OR      = exp(est),
    CI_low  = exp(ci[, 1]),
    CI_high = exp(ci[, 2]),
    row.names = NULL
  )
}

or_m4 <- get_or_table(m4)

tidy(m4, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(across(c(estimate, conf.low, conf.high), ~ round(., 3)),
         p.value = round(p.value, 3),
         sig = case_when(
           p.value < .001 ~ "***",
           p.value < .01  ~ "**",
           p.value < .05  ~ "*",
           p.value < .1   ~ ".",
           TRUE           ~ ""
         )) %>%
  rename(OR = estimate, CI_low = conf.low, CI_high = conf.high) %>%
  kable()
term OR std.error statistic p.value CI_low CI_high sig
(Intercept) 0.322 0.4981169 -2.2762594 0.023 0.114 0.824 *
z_neigh_disadv 1.364 0.1323289 2.3452230 0.019 1.054 1.773 *
gender2 0.750 0.1976409 -1.4527534 0.146 0.510 1.107
z_age 0.848 0.1315568 -1.2529940 0.210 0.655 1.098
incomeBelow Median (<$40k) 0.796 0.2023556 -1.1245836 0.261 0.536 1.185
raceBlack 4.836 0.5420322 2.9078005 0.004 1.728 14.752 **
raceOther 1.705 0.6101816 0.8747347 0.382 0.524 5.842
raceWhite 2.532 0.4889590 1.8996960 0.057 1.008 7.029 .
z_yoe 1.002 0.1025186 0.0177313 0.986 0.817 1.224
z_reward 1.368 0.1172504 2.6740868 0.007 1.089 1.727 **
z_dospert_hs 0.884 0.1833808 -0.6712243 0.502 0.615 1.264
z_dospert_soc 0.967 0.1807243 -0.1841705 0.854 0.678 1.378
z_dospert_ethic 1.032 0.2173926 0.1441018 0.885 0.673 1.581
z_dospert_inv 1.256 0.1784415 1.2778539 0.201 0.887 1.787
z_dospert_rec 1.408 0.1881867 1.8189150 0.069 0.976 2.043 .
z_depression 1.052 0.1615859 0.3107805 0.756 0.764 1.442
z_mania 1.124 0.1324128 0.8861204 0.376 0.867 1.458
z_anxiety 0.831 0.1522899 -1.2155853 0.224 0.615 1.118
z_loneliness 0.871 0.1240347 -1.1095952 0.267 0.682 1.109
z_planfulness 0.832 0.1157473 -1.5876080 0.112 0.662 1.043
z_fevs 0.994 0.1224477 -0.0471731 0.962 0.781 1.264
z_usidep 0.936 0.1123352 -0.5867823 0.557 0.750 1.166 
or_m4 %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(term = reorder(term, OR)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(OR, term)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = CI_low, xmax = CI_high), height = 0.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3, colour = col_pg) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(x = "Odds Ratio (log scale)", y = NULL,
       title = "Odds Ratios — HC vs. Recreational Gamblers (M4)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 6. ROC / AUC
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

col_ng <- "pink3"
col_rg <- "purple1"

roc1 <- roc(dat$PG, predict(m1, type = "response"), quiet = TRUE)
roc2 <- roc(dat$PG, predict(m2, type = "response"), quiet = TRUE)
roc3 <- roc(dat$PG, predict(m3, type = "response"), quiet = TRUE)
roc4 <- roc(dat$PG, predict(m4, type = "response"), quiet = TRUE)

auc_table <- data.frame(
  Model = c("M1: Context", "M2: + Reward/Risk",
            "M3: + Clinical", "M4: + Financial"),
  AUC   = round(c(auc(roc1), auc(roc2), auc(roc3), auc(roc4)), 3)
)
kable(auc_table)
Model AUC
M1: Context 0.690
M2: + Reward/Risk 0.727
M3: + Clinical 0.738
M4: + Financial 0.739 
cat("\n── DeLong Tests ──\n")
## 
## ── DeLong Tests ──
cat("M1 vs M2:\n"); print(roc.test(roc1, roc2, method = "delong"))
## M1 vs M2:
## 
##  DeLong's test for two correlated ROC curves
## 
## data:  roc1 and roc2
## Z = -2.7153, p-value = 0.006621
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in AUC is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.06365863 -0.01028480
## sample estimates:
## AUC of roc1 AUC of roc2 
##   0.6897903   0.7267620
cat("M2 vs M3:\n"); print(roc.test(roc2, roc3, method = "delong"))
## M2 vs M3:
## 
##  DeLong's test for two correlated ROC curves
## 
## data:  roc2 and roc3
## Z = -1.8423, p-value = 0.06543
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in AUC is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.0229634521  0.0007107467
## sample estimates:
## AUC of roc1 AUC of roc2 
##   0.7267620   0.7378884
cat("M3 vs M4:\n"); print(roc.test(roc3, roc4, method = "delong"))
## M3 vs M4:
## 
##  DeLong's test for two correlated ROC curves
## 
## data:  roc3 and roc4
## Z = -0.4107, p-value = 0.6813
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in AUC is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.007289607  0.004763860
## sample estimates:
## AUC of roc1 AUC of roc2 
##   0.7378884   0.7391513
ggroc(
  list(
    "M1: Context"       = roc1,
    "M2: + Reward/Risk" = roc2,
    "M3: + Clinical"    = roc3,
    "M4: + Financial"   = roc4
  ),
  linewidth = 1
) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 1, slope = 1,
              linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60") +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("M1: Context"       = "grey70",
               "M2: + Reward/Risk" = col_ng,
               "M3: + Clinical"    = col_rg,
               "M4: + Financial"   = col_pg)
  ) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.25, y = 0.30, hjust = 0, size = 3.8,
           label = paste0(
             "AUC M1 = ", auc_table$AUC[1], "\n",
             "AUC M2 = ", auc_table$AUC[2], "\n",
             "AUC M3 = ", auc_table$AUC[3], "\n",
             "AUC M4 = ", auc_table$AUC[4]
           )) +
  labs(x = "Specificity", y = "Sensitivity", colour = "Model",
       title = "ROC Curves: HC vs. Recreational Gamblers") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

# ── Classification at Youden's J ────────────────────────────────────────────
best_thresh <- coords(roc4, "best",
                      ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity",
                              "specificity", "ppv", "npv"))
cat("\n── Optimal Cut-Point (Youden's J) ──\n")
## 
## ── Optimal Cut-Point (Youden's J) ──
print(best_thresh)
##           threshold sensitivity specificity       ppv       npv
## threshold 0.3182575   0.7613169   0.6005291 0.5505952 0.7964912
predicted_class <- ifelse(predict(m4, type = "response") > best_thresh$threshold, 1, 0)
cat("\n── Confusion Matrix ──\n")
## 
## ── Confusion Matrix ──
table(Predicted = predicted_class, Actual = dat$PG)
##          Actual
## Predicted   0   1
##         0 227  58
##         1 151 185
cat("Accuracy:", round(mean(predicted_class == dat$PG, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "\n")
## Accuracy: 0.663
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 7. CROSS-VALIDATION & LASSO
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

library(boot)
## 
## Attaching package: 'boot'
## The following object is masked from 'package:car':
## 
##     logit
library(glmnet)
## Warning: package 'glmnet' was built under R version 4.3.3
## Loading required package: Matrix
## 
## Attaching package: 'Matrix'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:tidyr':
## 
##     expand, pack, unpack
## Loaded glmnet 4.1-9
set.seed(42)
K <- 10
folds <- sample(rep(1:K, length.out = nrow(dat)))

cv_auc <- function(formula, data, folds, K) {
  held_out_probs <- numeric(nrow(data))
  for (k in 1:K) {
    train <- data[folds != k, ]
    test  <- data[folds == k, ]
    fit   <- glm(formula, data = train, family = binomial)
    held_out_probs[folds == k] <- predict(fit, newdata = test, type = "response")
  }
  return(auc(roc(data$PG, held_out_probs, quiet = TRUE)))
}

cv_auc_m1 <- cv_auc(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe,
  dat, folds, K)
cv_auc_m2 <- cv_auc(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic +
    z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec,
  dat, folds, K)
cv_auc_m3 <- cv_auc(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic +
    z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec +
    z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness +
    z_planfulness,
  dat, folds, K)
cv_auc_m4 <- cv_auc(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + income + race + z_yoe +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic +
    z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec +
    z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness +
    z_planfulness +
    z_fevs + z_usidep,
  dat, folds, K)

comparison <- data.frame(
  Model        = c("M1: Context", "M2: + Reward/Risk",
                    "M3: + Clinical", "M4: + Financial"),
  InSample_AUC = round(c(auc(roc1), auc(roc2), auc(roc3), auc(roc4)), 3),
  CV_AUC       = round(c(cv_auc_m1, cv_auc_m2, cv_auc_m3, cv_auc_m4), 3)
)
comparison$Shrinkage <- comparison$InSample_AUC - comparison$CV_AUC
kable(comparison)
Model InSample_AUC CV_AUC Shrinkage
M1: Context 0.690 0.671 0.019
M2: + Reward/Risk 0.727 0.697 0.030
M3: + Clinical 0.738 0.695 0.043
M4: + Financial 0.739 0.691 0.048 
# ── Repeated CV (M3 and M4 only) ────────────────────────────────────────────
n_repeats <- 50

repeated_cv_auc <- function(formula, data, K, n_repeats) {
  aucs <- numeric(n_repeats)
  for (r in 1:n_repeats) {
    folds_r <- sample(rep(1:K, length.out = nrow(data)))
    aucs[r] <- cv_auc(formula, data, folds_r, K)
  }
  return(aucs)
}

cat("\n── Running 50 × 10-fold CV ──\n")
## 
## ── Running 50 × 10-fold CV ──
rep_m3 <- repeated_cv_auc(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic +
    z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec +
    z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness +
    z_planfulness,
  dat, K, n_repeats)

rep_m4 <- repeated_cv_auc(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + income + race + z_yoe +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic +
    z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec +
    z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness +
    z_planfulness +
    z_fevs + z_usidep,
  dat, K, n_repeats)

cat(sprintf("  M3 repeated CV: %.3f [%.3f, %.3f]\n",
    mean(rep_m3), quantile(rep_m3, 0.025), quantile(rep_m3, 0.975)))
##   M3 repeated CV: 0.695 [0.686, 0.703]
cat(sprintf("  M4 repeated CV: %.3f [%.3f, %.3f]\n",
    mean(rep_m4), quantile(rep_m4, 0.025), quantile(rep_m4, 0.975)))
##   M4 repeated CV: 0.690 [0.682, 0.699]
# ── LASSO ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
x_vars <- model.matrix(
  ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + income + race + z_yoe +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic +
    z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec +
    z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness +
    z_planfulness + z_fevs + z_usidep,
  data = dat
)[, -1]

y <- dat$PG

set.seed(42)
lasso_cv_auc <- cv.glmnet(x_vars, y, family = "binomial",
                           alpha = 1, nfolds = 10, type.measure = "auc")

lasso_coefs <- coef(lasso_cv_auc, s = "lambda.1se")
retained <- data.frame(
  term = rownames(lasso_coefs),
  coef = round(as.numeric(lasso_coefs), 4)
) %>%
  filter(coef != 0, term != "(Intercept)")

cat("\n── LASSO Retained Predictors ──\n")
## 
## ── LASSO Retained Predictors ──
kable(retained)
term coef
z_reward 0.0843
z_dospert_ethic 0.1754
z_dospert_inv 0.0338
z_dospert_rec 0.0018 
best_idx <- which(lasso_cv_auc$lambda == lasso_cv_auc$lambda.min)
cat(sprintf("\n── LASSO CV AUC: %.3f ──\n", lasso_cv_auc$cvm[best_idx]))
## 
## ── LASSO CV AUC: 0.709 ──
# ── Summary ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("\n═══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
## 
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
cat("  OVERFITTING DIAGNOSTIC SUMMARY\n")
##   OVERFITTING DIAGNOSTIC SUMMARY
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
cat(sprintf("  M4 in-sample AUC:    %.3f\n", auc(roc4)))
##   M4 in-sample AUC:    0.739
cat(sprintf("  M4 10-fold CV AUC:   %.3f\n", cv_auc_m4))
##   M4 10-fold CV AUC:   0.691
cat(sprintf("  M4 repeated CV:      %.3f [%.3f, %.3f]\n",
    mean(rep_m4), quantile(rep_m4, 0.025), quantile(rep_m4, 0.975)))
##   M4 repeated CV:      0.690 [0.682, 0.699]
cat(sprintf("  LASSO CV AUC:        %.3f\n", lasso_cv_auc$cvm[best_idx]))
##   LASSO CV AUC:        0.709
cat(sprintf("  Predictors retained: %d / %d\n", nrow(retained), ncol(x_vars)))
##   Predictors retained: 4 / 22
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
